---
title: "Reproducible examples: a love letter"
subtitle: "The best way to answer your own questions"
author: "Martin Frigaard"
date: "2026-03-05"
categories: [R, Python, Markdown]
# image: "img/image.png"
toc: true
toc-depth: 5
toc-title: 'Contents'
toc-location: "left"
# code-block-border-left: true
code-block-bg: "#f8f8f8"
code-block-border-left: "#e8e8e8"
code-fold: show
code-summary: 'show/hide'
callout-icon: false

draft: false

freeze: true

knitr:
  opts_chunk: 
    fig-dpi: 320
    fig-width: 6
    out-width: '85%'
    fig-format: png
    fig-align: 'center'

execute:
  echo: true
  message: false
  warning: false
  eval: false
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| eval: true 
#| echo: false 
#| include: false
source("../_common.R")
options(
  scipen = 999,
  repos = c(pm = "https://packagemanager.posit.co/cran/latest",
            CRAN = "https://cloud.r-project.org")
  )
library(quarto)
library(rmarkdown)
library(shiny)
library(lobstr)
```

```{r}
#| label: co_box_dev
#| echo: false
#| results: asis
#| eval: true
co_box(color = "r", 
  header = "DRAFT!", 
  contents = "This post is currently under development--thank you for your patience.")
```

With developers using LLMs for coding questions, are reproducible examples (reprexes) still relevant? It might seem easier just to ask an AI, but I believe reprexes remain valuable—and we should keep finding ways to make them better.

Most people write a reprex to ask about a problem. Sometimes it's an error or warning, but often the code just gives the wrong result. With LLMs generating code, these mistakes are even easier to miss—they may not show up until you test the output or something else fails.

Problems without errors or warnings are worse—they look like they work, but no one knows what the code does or if it’s correct.

```{r}
#| label: co_box_reprex
#| echo: false
#| results: asis
#| eval: true
co_box(color = "g", 
  header = "Packages for reproducible examples", 
  contents = "

-   The [`reprex` package](https://reprex.tidyverse.org/) is designed to '*to encourage the sharing of small, reproducible, and runnable examples on code-oriented websites*.' 

-   The [`reprexpy` package](https://reprexpy.readthedocs.io/en/latest/) is a similar package for Python.")
```

Let's consider the following example:

*We wrote code, executed it, and an error was returned*

```{mermaid}
%%| eval: true
%%| echo: false
%%{init: {'theme': 'neutral', 'themeVariables': { 'fontFamily': 'monospace', 'fontSize':'18px'}}}%%

graph TD
    WriteCode(["Write Code"])
    ExecCode(["Execute Code"])
    
    WriteCode --> ExecCode
    
    ExecCode --> ErrPath{"<strong>Error<br>Returned?</strong>"}
    
    ErrPath -.->|"Yes"| Reprex(["<strong>Reproducible<br>Example</strong>"])
    ErrPath -->|"No"| ResultCheck{"<strong>Expected<br>Result?</strong>"}
    
    ResultCheck -->|"Yes"| AllGood(["<strong>Expected<br>Output</strong>"])
    ResultCheck -->|"No"| SilentFail["<strong>Silent Failure</strong><br/><i>Wrong result,<br> no error</i>"]
    ResultCheck -->|"Unknown"| NobodyKnows["<strong>Unverifiable</strong><br/><i>No one knows what<br/>it should produce</i>"]
    
    %% ErrPath -.-> Reprex(["<strong>Reproducible Example</strong>"])

    style NobodyKnows fill:#D32F2F,color:#fff
    style SilentFail fill:#ffa500,color:#fff
    style AllGood fill:#388E3C,color:#fff
    style Reprex fill:#4051B5,color:#fff
    
```

## Embracing minimalism

The real art in creating a good reprex is identifying the code that isn't needed to reproduce the error (including packages, function calls, objects, etc). Deleting code can be painful, especially if you've already made multiple attempts to address the error. But winnowing down code gives us an opportunity to think clearly and deeply about the problem we've encountered.

```{r}
#| label: co_box_rewriting
#| echo: false
#| results: asis
#| eval: true
co_box(color = "g", 
  header = "Revision = open-mindedness", 
  contents = "
> '*rewriting is the key to improved thinking. It demands a real open-mindedness and objectivity.*'")
```




```{r}
#| label: co_box_verbiage
#| echo: false
#| results: asis
#| eval: true
co_box(color = "g", 
  header = "Revision = clarity", 
  contents = "
> '*[revision] demands a willingness to cull verbiage so that ideas stand out clearly. And it demands a willingness to meet logical contradictions head on and trace them to the premises that have created them.*'")
```



```{r}
#| label: co_box_courage
#| echo: false
#| results: asis
#| eval: true
co_box(color = "g", 
  header = "Revision = courage", 
  contents = "
> '*In short, it forces a writer to get up his courage and expose his thinking process to his own intelligence.*' - Marvin H. Swift, [Clear Writing Means Clear Thinking Means…](https://hbr.org/1973/01/clear-writing-means-clear-thinking-means)
  ")
```
